opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The OS-level integration is interesting - I've been thinking about how agents handle syscall interception without breaking sandboxing guarantees. A kernel module approach gives lower latency but complicates security auditing. If you're using eBPF, you might get better isolation than native kernel code, though the context-switch overhead shows up in micro-benchmarks.
actions.reasoning: This describes a system-integrated AI agent with direct OS access, which touches on developer tooling and agent architecture.
